name: Ben Rich Statements
description: The 'Ben Rich Statements' refer to alleged deathbed confessions attributed to Ben Rich, former CEO of Lockheed Martin's Skunk Works. Proponents claim Rich admitted that the United States military has possessed technology capable of interstellar travel for decades, and that this technology was derived from extraterrestrial sources. These claims often circulate within UFO enthusiast communities and alternative history circles. The alleged statements often include details about reverse-engineering alien spacecraft and the existence of antigravity propulsion systems. The origin of these claims is primarily traced back to accounts from individuals who claim to have spoken with Rich shortly before his death in 1995, particularly Jan Harzan (Executive Director of MUFON). Mainstream scientific and engineering communities generally dismiss these claims as lacking credible evidence. They point to the absence of verifiable documentation, the reliance on anecdotal accounts, and the contradiction with known principles of physics and engineering. Lockheed Martin has not officially commented on the specific claims, but generally refutes suggestions of possessing technology beyond current understanding. Critics also note the potential for misinterpretations, embellishments, or outright fabrications in the retelling of Rich's alleged statements, especially given the lack of direct recordings or written documentation. The statements fuel ongoing speculation about government secrecy, advanced aerospace technology, and extraterrestrial contact, but remain firmly outside the realm of established scientific consensus.
subtitle: Alleged confessions of advanced aerospace secrets

claims.claim: The US military possesses technology capable of interstellar travel.
claims.evidence: Alleged statements from Ben Rich before his death.
claims.criticism: Lack of verifiable evidence, contradiction with known physics, reliance on anecdotal accounts.

claims.claim: This technology was derived from reverse-engineering extraterrestrial spacecraft.
claims.evidence: Attributed claims of alien technology acquisition and reverse engineering.
claims.criticism: Absence of physical evidence of alien spacecraft, implausibility of successful reverse-engineering without fundamental understanding.

claims.claim: The technology includes antigravity propulsion systems.
claims.criticism: Antigravity remains a theoretical concept with no proven existence, no credible scientific evidence of such technology being developed.

keyFigures.name: Jan Harzan
keyFigures.role: Executive Director of MUFON, reported Rich's statements

keyFigures.name: Stanton Friedman
keyFigures.role: Nuclear physicist and ufologist, promoted the claims

keyFigures.name: Philip J. Klass
keyFigures.role: UFO skeptic, criticized the claims as unsubstantiated

mythologyConnections: Chariots of the Gods (ancient astronaut theory)

timeline.event: Dissemination of alleged 'Ben Rich statements' within UFO communities
Continued debate and speculation regarding the veracity of the claims

sources.author: Various UFO Researchers
sources.work: Numerous online articles and forum discussions
sources.title: Various UFO Researchers

sources.author: Paul Czysz and Claudio Bruno
sources.work: Future Spacecraft Propulsion Systems: Enabling Technologies for Space Exploration
